# Transcrição: 03- Humor 3 - One of These Things

- **Fonte:** 03- Humor 3 - One of These Things
- **Duração:** 01:07
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:07]

One of these things is not like the other. This is a game that was played on Sesame Street for years to teach children how to categorize. Essentially what you do is you take a list of three things. Two of them are going to be in one category and the third one is going to be so far afield from the other two that it's going to make people laugh. My favorite example comes from a storyteller named Steve Zimmer, my favorite storyteller of all time. He's telling a story about how in his neighborhood as a child, all of his neighbors hosted a Hawaiian luau, but his family was not invited to the party. So the next weekend, his family, the Zimmer family had their own luau and he says it featured pineapples, Hawaiian punch and despair. It makes everyone laugh because Hawaiian punch and pineapples are expected items at a Hawaiian luau. Despair is not, but even funnier than it not being expected at a Hawaiian luau, it doesn't match the other two things that came before it.
